trigger_eval.method: manual prediction (HOUSECARL_SKILL_AUTHORING.md §6.5 fallback; empirical run_loop needs a non-Windows host, session is Windows 11)
trigger_eval.followup: Flagged for empirical run_loop re-validation on a non-Windows host + one independent peer prediction check (§6.5 items 3-4). The riskiest false (RACE patch sharing 'balance ... for Requiem') and the riskiest true (caster balance vs spell design) are called out in the eval notes.

round_trip.method: Humanoid, blind tier-extrapolation: from the melee bandit tiers 01/02/04 (Level 3/7/12, perks 5/9/12, outfit 9336AC/AD/AF), blind-derive tier 05's balance fields, then diff vs the houseCARL winner.

predicted.Flags: Respawn, AutoCalcStats, LoopedScript, LoopedAudio (constant); fixed level, no PCLevelMult

round_trip.notes: Strong match. All constants exact. Level predicted ~18-19 -> actual 19 (exact). Outfit predicted 9336B0 (sequential) -> exact. Template predicted in the 899DD7+ series -> actual 899DD8 (sequential, exact). Perks 18 within the predicted escalation. Confirms the fixed-level tier-ladder method: a tier shares class/style/race/deathitem/flags and escalates only level + perk-count + gear + template base. Tier 06 cross-check (Level 24, perks 18, outfit 9336B1, template 899DD9) corroborates the gradient.

round_trip.method: Creature, recognized-race trait-perk confirmation: verify the Reqtificator-added racial trait perk is ABSENT from a source draugr NPC and that its perks are vanilla combat perks.

predicted.trait_perk: draugr incomingDamageModifier 031285:Requiem.esp should be ABSENT from source (Reqtificator adds it at build by race match)
predicted.actoreffect: the race's armor trait is engine-inherited, NOT replicated as a perk

actual.flags: Respawn (no AutoCalcStats), HealthOffset 200, StaminaOffset 100, Level 6 fixed

round_trip.notes: Confirms the perk split: the 14 source perks are all vanilla combat perks; the draugr trait perk 031285 is NOT among them -> Reqtificator-added at build by race-FormID match. So a modded draugr on the recognized draugr race needs only its level/offsets/combat perks; never hand-stamp 031285. Requiem's EncWolf being identical-to-vanilla and EncTroll adding just one perk corroborate 'recognized creature -> touch almost nothing'.

round_trip.method: Boss profile confirmation: verify a Requiem dragon-priest boss carries a high fixed level, a large caster stat offset, a Requiem trait spell, and a Requiem skill perk (the boss-buffing pattern).

predicted.trait: a REQ_Trait_* on ActorEffect for signature toughness
predicted.named_unique: templates onto the generic boss for stats/spell-list, keeps own perks

actual.EncDragonPriest: Level 50 fixed, Flags Respawn+BleedoutOverride, MagickaOffset 1300, 15 perks (14 vanilla + REQ_Conjuration_Empower_050 185736), ActorEffect = REQ_Trait_ResistMagic30 ADDDC7 + priest spells
actual.Rahgot: Flags Unique+BleedoutOverride, Level 50, MagickaOffset 1300, Template -> EncDragonPriestFrost 02025A with TemplateFlags Traits/Stats/Factions/SpellList, keeps own 11 perks + REQ_Trait_ResistMagic30 + signature spell

round_trip.notes: Confirms the boss pattern baked into the skill: high fixed level (50), large role stat offset (MagickaOffset 1300 for a caster), a Requiem skill perk, and a reusable REQ_Trait_* (ResistMagic30 ADDDC7) on the ActorEffect for signature toughness. The named unique (Rahgot) templates onto the generic boss for stats/spell-list while keeping its own perks + trait -- the 'set fields directly / reuse-or-author a REQ_Trait' rule, verified.
